description: Fix Calendar MCP integration - Add error logging, timeout handling, and debug output to the scheduler agent flow so calendar data issues are diagnosable
steps: In backend/app/mcp/client.py: Add 'import logging' at top, create logger = logging.getLogger(__name__). In the call_tool method, wrap the existing `await server.session.call_tool(tool_name, arguments)` call with asyncio.wait_for using a 30-second timeout. Catch asyncio.TimeoutError and raise RuntimeError with descriptive message. Add logging.info before calling the tool (log server_name, tool_name) and logging.info/error after (log success/failure).

In backend/app/agents/base.py: Add 'import logging' at top, create logger = logging.getLogger(__name__). In call_mcp_tool method, add logger.info before calling self.mcp_client.call_tool (log server_name, tool_name, agent name). Wrap in try/except, log exceptions with logger.error and re-raise.

In backend/app/agents/scheduler.py: Add 'import logging' at top, create logger = logging.getLogger(__name__). In _list_events: replace bare 'except Exception: return []' with 'except Exception as e: logger.error(f"[scheduler] _list_events failed: {e}", exc_info=True); return []'. Do the same for _find_free_slots and _create_event. In the execute method, add logger.info statements showing the action being performed and whether MCP client/auth_token are available.

In backend/app/agents/orchestrator.py: Add 'import logging' at top, create logger = logging.getLogger(__name__). In the execute method, add logger.info when routing to an agent (log agent_name, instruction). After the agent.execute call, log the result content length and agent name. In the exception fallback of _analyze_intent, log the actual exception.
acceptance_criteria: backend/app/mcp/client.py call_tool has asyncio.wait_for with 30s timeout wrapping session.call_tool
All four backend files have proper logging imports and logger instances
scheduler.py logs exceptions with exc_info=True in all three MCP-calling methods
orchestrator.py logs routing decisions (which agent, what instruction) and results
Python syntax is valid (python -c 'import ast; ast.parse(open(file).read())' passes for all modified files)
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ChronAI/backend && python -c "import ast; [ast.parse(open(f'app/agents/{f}').read()) for f in ['scheduler.py','base.py','orchestrator.py']]; ast.parse(open('app/mcp/client.py').read()); print('All files parse OK')"
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ChronAI/backend && grep -n 'asyncio.wait_for' app/mcp/client.py to confirm timeout is present
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ChronAI/backend && grep -n 'logger.error' app/agents/scheduler.py to confirm error logging
findings: All four files modified successfully. Python AST verification passes. Used python3 (not python) for verification as pyenv doesn't have a default python set.
